description: Students provide examples of attendance, punctuality, and reliability, then write statements demonstrating workplace readiness.

rubric.description: Student provides three clear, specific examples of strong attendance habits. Examples should show consistency, responsibility, and awareness of workplace expectations.
Student writes a polished, professional attendance statement that reflects reliability, commitment, and readiness for employment.
Student provides three detailed examples demonstrating punctuality, preparation, and respect for schedules.
Student writes a professional punctuality statement that communicates dependability and workplace readiness.
Student provides three examples showing reliability, follow-through, accountability, and willingness to support team needs.
Student writes a strong reliability statement that demonstrates readiness for employment and ability to be trusted with responsibilities.

aiPrompt: You are an employment readiness grader. The student's name is {{studentName}}. Evaluate their attendance, punctuality, and reliability examples and statements. Score each rubric category based on clarity, specificity, and professionalism. Provide a suggested total score out of 60, then scale it to a 10-point assignment. Then generate 4 comments: three detailed, positive comments highlighting specific strengths in {{studentName}}'s work, and one constructive comment explaining exactly where points were lost and how {{studentName}} can improve. Comments must be rich, specific, and written in a supportive, employer-style tone. Avoid generic praise. Reference the student's examples directly when possible.
suggestedComments: Your attendance examples show a strong understanding of workplace expectations, {{studentName}}. You clearly demonstrate consistency and responsibility, which are qualities employers value highly.

Your punctuality examples are well chosen and show excellent time management, {{studentName}}. You highlight not just being on time, but preparing ahead and respecting schedules — this is exactly what employers look for.
Your reliability examples demonstrate that you can be trusted to follow through on commitments, {{studentName}}. You show initiative, accountability, and a willingness to support others.
Your statements are written in a professional tone and would make a strong impression in an interview, {{studentName}}.

To strengthen your work further, {{studentName}}, consider adding more detail or specific situations to show the full impact of your actions. Employers appreciate examples that describe not just what you did, but how it made a difference.
